01_initial_request.target_name: Confidence regions for Cauchy distribution parameters
01_initial_request.user_intent: To construct explicit confidence regions for Cauchy distribution parameters using geometric mean and complex plane analysis, overcoming limitations of maximum likelihood estimators
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Confidence disc in the complex plane

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The Cauchy distribution has location parameter μ and scale parameter σ that need to be estimated simultaneously. [Gap] No explicit confidence regions for Cauchy distributions are known, and maximum likelihood estimators lack closed-form formulas for sample sizes ≥5. [Decision] To achieve confidence region construction, chose to use Cauchy integral formula to perform expectation calculations on complex parameter γ=μ+iσ, expecting to obtain E[X^p]=γ^p for |p|<1.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Derived E[X^p]=γ^p and E[(log X)^p]=(log γ)^p, establishing geometric mean as unbiased estimator for γ

references.relevance: Proposed treating location and scale parameters as single complex parameter γ=μ+iσ

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Expectation formulas for Cauchy distribution have been derived. [Gap] Need to compute variance of log X to establish central limit theorem. [Decision] To achieve variance calculation, chose to use mathematical derivation (Proposition 2.2) to perform moment generating function analysis on log|X|, expecting to obtain variance expression v=2 arg γ(π-arg γ).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained Var(log X)=2 arg γ(π-arg γ) and established that log X - log γ is proper complex random variable

references.relevance: Provided explicit formula for E[|X|^p] though derivation was complicated

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variance of log X has been computed. [Gap] Need to establish central limit theorem for geometric mean to construct confidence regions. [Decision] To achieve CLT formulation, chose to use complex delta-method (Lemma 3.2) to perform transformation on logarithms of Cauchy variables with f(z)=e^z, expecting to obtain CLT for geometric mean.

parameters.domain: holomorphic function in domain containing m∈ℂ

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Established Lemma 3.3: √N(P_N-γ)/γ converges to standard complex normal as N→∞

references.relevance: Provided complex version of delta-method for statistical inference

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CLT for geometric mean has been established but cannot directly yield confidence disc. [Gap] Need consistent estimator for variance to make CLT applicable for confidence region. [Decision] To achieve confidence region construction, chose to use consistent estimator V_N (Lemma 3.4) to perform variance estimation on sample, expecting to obtain modified CLT suitable for confidence disc.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained Lemma 3.4: √N(P_N-γ)/√(V_N/2) converges to standard complex normal, enabling confidence disc construction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Modified CLT with consistent variance estimator is now available. [Gap] Need explicit formula for confidence region in complex plane. [Decision] To achieve confidence disc formula, chose to use Theorem 3.1 to perform asymptotic confidence region derivation with confidence level α, expecting to obtain disc B(P_N, R_α√(V_N/N)).

parameters.disc_definition: B(p,r) denotes disc in ℂ with center p and radius r

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Derived confidence disc: lim P(γ∈B(P_N, R_α√(V_N/N))) = 1-α as N→∞

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Confidence disc formula has been derived. [Gap] Alternative confidence region shapes may provide tighter bounds. [Decision] To achieve confidence square formula, chose to use Corollary 3.1 to perform square region derivation with quantile ρ_β, expecting to obtain smaller confidence square than disc.

parameters.square_definition: Q(p,r) with center p and side length 2r
parameters.normal_quantile: ρ_α is upper α-quantile of standard real normal

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Derived confidence square somewhat smaller than disc with same coverage probability

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Confidence disc and square formulas are theoretically established. [Gap] Need to validate method with numerical examples. [Decision] To achieve numerical validation, chose to use Monte Carlo simulation to perform confidence disc generation with μ=0, σ=1, N=1000, expecting to obtain 10 trials showing true value containment.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 10 confidence discs; symbols ○ indicate centers containing true value (0,1), ▽ indicates failure point

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] First numerical example with μ=0 completed. [Gap] Need to test method with larger location parameter values. [Decision] To achieve parameter range validation, chose to use Monte Carlo simulation to perform confidence disc generation with μ=5, σ=1, expecting to observe radius increase with |μ|.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Var(log X)≈1.162; radius of confidence discs increases as |μ| increases despite smaller variance

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Large sample size (N=1000) examples completed. [Gap] Need to test method performance with small sample sizes where CLT approximation may fail. [Decision] To achieve small sample validation, chose to use Monte Carlo simulation to perform confidence disc generation with N=30, expecting to observe coverage rate deviation from nominal 95%.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Only 925 trials out of 1000 contain true value (92.5% coverage vs nominal 95%), showing CLT approximation limitation for small N

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Standard confidence disc method tested across parameter ranges. [Gap] When |μ|>>σ, all data may have same sign causing imaginary part of geometric mean to vanish. [Decision] To achieve improved estimation for large μ, chose to use median subtraction preprocessing to perform data transformation Y_i=X_i-med(X_1,...,X_N), expecting to obtain balanced positive/negative values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Subtracting median decreases absolute value of geometric mean, making estimated discs smaller; however transformed data are neither Cauchy nor independent

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Confidence disc method validated for Cauchy distributions. [Gap] Need to demonstrate robustness advantage over Gaussian-based methods when outliers present. [Decision] To achieve outlier robustness comparison, chose to use t-distribution confidence interval vs Cauchy method (Corollary 3.2) to perform contamination experiment with 10 samples of 100 values each, expecting to show Cauchy method less affected by outliers.

parameters.estimators: AM (arithmetic mean) and GM (geometric mean real part)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Confidence intervals from Corollary 3.2 not strongly affected by outliers compared to t-distribution method

references.relevance: Previous work on confidence regions using maximum likelihood estimator related to likelihood-ratio test

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Monte Carlo simulation with multiple parameter configurations and outlier robustness comparison

coverage_rate_large_sample.interpretation: Nominal coverage achieved for N=1000 with α=0.05

coverage_rate_small_sample.interpretation: 925/1000 trials contain true value for N=30, showing CLT approximation limitation

variance_log_X_mu5.interpretation: Var(log X) when μ=5, σ=1, calculated as 2 arg γ(π-arg γ)

outlier_robustness.interpretation: Cauchy method confidence intervals less affected by outliers than t-distribution method

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Successfully achieved objective: constructed explicit confidence regions (disc and square) for Cauchy distribution parameters using geometric mean and complex plane analysis. Method overcomes MLE limitations by providing closed-form formulas applicable for any sample size N≥2, with validated coverage rates and demonstrated outlier robustness.
